---
title: "blueterra"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{blueterra}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>", fig.width = 7, fig.height = 4)
```

```{r setup, message = FALSE}
library(blueterra)
library(terra)
```

`blueterra` supports geomorphometric analysis of submerged terrain from
bathymetric and elevation rasters. The workflow stays close to `terra` objects:
rasters remain `SpatRaster` objects, zones and transects remain `SpatVector`
objects, and derived metrics can be summarized as ordinary tabular outputs.

<img src="../man/figures/study-area-pr-southwest-shelf-margin.png" width="100%" alt="Study-area context for the example data along the southwest Puerto Rico shelf margin." />

The examples use reduced bathymetry and sampling rectangles from
Hole-in-the-Wall, El Hoyo, and a broader slope clip along the southwest Puerto
Rico shelf margin near La Parguera, Puerto Rico. The files are compact, but
they retain real relief and depth gradients.

## Read Example Rasters

```{r read}
hitw <- read_bathy(blueterra_example("hitw"))
hoyo <- read_bathy(blueterra_example("hoyo"))
rectangles <- terra::vect(blueterra_example("sampling_rectangles"))
hitw_rect <- rectangles[rectangles$site_id == "hitw", ]

bathy_info(hitw)
rectangles[, c("site_id", "site_name", "feature_type")]
```

`bathy_info()` is a first check on whether the surface has the dimensions, value
range, cell size, and CRS expected for the analysis.

## Prepare the Surface

The example bathymetry stores depth as negative elevation. The sign is
preserved during preparation.

```{r prepare}
prepared <- prepare_bathy(
  hitw,
  depth_range = c(-220, -25),
  smooth = TRUE,
  smooth_window = 3
)
check_bathy_units(prepared, units = "m", positive_depth = FALSE)
range(terra::values(prepared), na.rm = TRUE)
```

Depth conversion is explicit when a positive-depth convention is needed.

```{r signs}
positive_depth <- set_depth_positive(prepared)
range(terra::values(positive_depth), na.rm = TRUE)
```

## Map the Surface

Hillshade is a visual relief layer. It helps the reader see escarpments and
local relief, but it is not treated as a predictor unless the user explicitly
derives and analyzes it.

```{r map, eval=requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE), fig.alt="Hole-in-the-Wall bathymetry with hillshade and contours."}
plot_bathy(
  prepared,
  contours = TRUE,
  contour_interval = 25,
  vectors = hitw_rect,
  title = "Hole-in-the-Wall Bathymetry",
  subtitle = "Hillshade, contours, and sampling rectangle"
)
```

## Derive Terrain Metrics

Terrain derivatives describe different aspects of the same surface. Slope and
aspect describe local orientation, TRI and rugosity describe local relief
variability, BPI describes relative position, and curvature summarizes local
surface bending.

```{r metrics}
terrain <- derive_terrain(
  prepared,
  metrics = c("slope", "aspect", "northness", "eastness", "tri", "bpi",
              "curvature", "surface_area_ratio")
)
names(terrain)
```

```{r metric-map, eval=requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE), fig.alt="Slope over hillshaded bathymetry."}
plot_metric(
  terrain,
  "slope_deg",
  bathy = prepared,
  contours = TRUE,
  contour_interval = 25,
  title = "Slope Over Hillshade"
)
```

## Summarize Sampling Rectangles

```{r zones}
summary <- summarize_terrain(
  terrain,
  hitw_rect,
  fun = c("mean", "sd", "min", "max")
)
summary[, c("site_id", "site_name", "slope_deg_mean", "bpi_3x3_mean")]
```

## Depth Bands, Transects, and Isobaths

These summaries answer different spatial questions. Depth bands compare
vertical intervals, transects show cross-sectional profiles, and isobath
corridors compare terrain along depth horizons.

```{r sections}
summarize_depth_bands(
  prepared,
  metrics = terrain,
  breaks = c(-220, -150, -100, -60, -30, -20)
)

transects <- make_transects(hitw_rect, spacing = 75)
samples <- sample_transects(transects, prepared, n = 12)
summarize_cross_sections(samples)

corridors <- make_isobath_corridors(prepared, depths = c(-50, -80, -120), width = 20)
summarize_isobath_terrain(terrain, corridors)
```

```{r corridor-map, eval=requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE), fig.alt="Isobath corridors over hillshaded bathymetry."}
plot_isobath_corridors(corridors, prepared, contour_interval = 25)
```

## Model-Ready Tables

```{r model-ready}
cells <- sample_terrain_cells(terrain, size = 40, method = "regular")
terrain_correlation(cells[, c("slope_deg", "tri", "bpi_3x3", "curvature")])
terrain_pca(cells[, c("slope_deg", "tri", "bpi_3x3", "curvature")])$variance
```
